# Taylor (NotGreg) — Relationship Dynamics — February 19, 2026

---

## Where Things Stand

The relationship is progressing. Taylor showed up again, brought Hannah in (a signal he's treating this seriously), and led with useful feedback rather than another pitch. He hasn't pushed on anything Dax said no to — bookmaking and Stripe haven't come back up. The PA connection (both tied to Collegeville) made things feel more personal and less transactional.

---

## Dax's Read on Taylor at This Point

Consistent. He does what he says he'll do, doesn't push where he's been declined, and is genuinely interested in a long-term relationship rather than a quick transaction. The consulting feedback he gave on DD (give less away for free, charge for the engagement) was substantive and not self-serving — he had nothing to gain from saying it.

The salesman tendencies are still there, but they're softer in this call. More listening, less pitching.

---

## What Taylor Actually Wants From Dax

Same as before, now clearer:
1. **Discord building knowledge** — he's actively trying to build a music/concert community and needs the operational knowledge Dax has
2. **Long-term relationship** — he explicitly sees Dax as someone he wants in his circle for the long run; the "little brother" framing is real, not just flattery
3. **Referral pipeline** — still on the table but not pushed this call

---

## What to Watch

- **Credibility still unverified.** Taylor mentioned sending company links after the previous call. It's unclear whether those were sent or followed up on. Don't advance to any formal arrangement until that's confirmed and reviewed.
- **Sumer Labs pitch.** Taylor now wants to connect his dev partners to Dax's internship company. This is fine to facilitate via calendar link but don't over-commit — Dax is an intern, not a decision-maker there.
- **Communication volume.** Taylor warned again that he overwhelms people. He sends many short messages instead of one long one. Set expectations early if it becomes an issue.

---

## How to Approach Future Conversations

- Lead with the credibility check — has he sent those company links yet?
- Banking conversation: review the links when they arrive, no rush to decide
- Keep the Discord walkthrough casual — a screen share when Dax has 30–60 minutes free, not a formal session
- The fintech intro is a low-cost give — send the calendar link and let it play out
- No need to force pace on this relationship; Taylor is patient and will stay in touch
